name: the-day-of-the-jackal
description: Frederick Forsyth's The Day of the Jackal — a classic thriller about planning, attention to detail, and the cat-and-mouse between a professional assassin and the detective hunting him.
useCase: High-stakes planning, attention to detail, moving unseen, outthinking an opponent, performing under pressure.
knownRisks: The novel romanticizes a professional assassin — distinguish the method from the morality
The plot involves an attempt to kill a real historical figure
Some planning details are presented as cool competence — use judgment in applying them
